refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation end-to-end communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms under agreed network conditions to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly state the latency metric and its impact on safe teleoperation, clarifying the performance requirement and safety criticality.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms; perform safety validation with teleoperation scenarios.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link quality to support latency requirements.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking data exchange with minimal delay.

candidate_dps: DP1: Optimize data encoding and compression algorithms to reduce payload size.
DP2: Use dedicated low-latency network protocols and QoS prioritization.
DP3: Implement edge computing nodes to process telemetry/tasking closer to vehicle.
DP4: Design a streamlined communication stack minimizing buffering and retransmissions.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP2: Use dedicated low-latency network protocols and QoS prioritization.

potential_sources: Interaction between network protocol optimization and telemetry data format.
Dependency on network link quality and vehicle communication hardware.
coupling_check.notes: DP2 focuses on network protocol and QoS, which logically isolates latency control from telemetry data content, minimizing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network infrastructure supports QoS and low-latency protocols.
Risk that network conditions degrade beyond agreed parameters affecting latency.
Assumes vehicle communication hardware can support selected protocols.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating edge computing (DP3) if network conditions are variable or insufficient.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Responsible for implementing communication protocols, managing network link, and ensuring latency targets for teleoperation.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface to physical and logical network infrastructure enabling data exchange with vehicle and NGHE solution.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Real-time telemetry data exchanged between vehicle and teleoperation module.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands and control tasking data exchanged for teleoperation control.

module_containment_scores.notes: High-level system module; contains coordination but not low-level latency control.
Vehicle hardware/software involved but latency protocol logic better centralized in Teleoperation.
Primary module responsible for latency-sensitive communication protocols and network management.
Safety monitoring but not responsible for latency control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Latency control logic and network protocol management are best contained within the Teleoperation module to minimize diffusion and maximize ownership clarity.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on vehicle hardware capabilities may require cross-module coordination.
Network link variability may require interfaces with NGHE Solution for fallback or monitoring.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts with Vehicle and NGHE Solution to manage cross-module dependencies.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (low-latency network protocols and QoS) directly addresses the refined FR of meeting latency requirements with clear assumptions and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists if vehicle hardware constraints require protocol adjustments.
Cross-module coordination needed for network link variability.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module has highest containment score and ownership clarity for DP implementation.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be measured end-to-end across Teleoperation module interfaces; ownership boundaries are clear for testing and validation.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: FR is clearly allocated to Teleoperation IM with well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Clarify interface contracts with Vehicle and NGHE Solution modules.
Plan for fallback or monitoring integration with Safety Services for safety-critical alerts.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and allocation meets the requirements with clear FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment. Verification and ownership boundaries are well defined with manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link quality to support latency requirements.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking data exchange with minimal delay.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level for detailed sub-FRs.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports QoS and low-latency protocols.
Vehicle hardware supports required communication protocols.
open_risks: Network condition variability beyond agreed parameters.
Cross-module dependencies on vehicle hardware capabilities.
evidence_needed: Latency performance measurements under representative network conditions.
Validation of protocol implementation in Teleoperation module.
